title: AZ-900 Mock Exam 2: Applied Azure Scenarios
description: A 50-question unofficial mock paper aligned to the AZ-900 skills measured from July 20, 2026. Uses realistic dropdown, statement, single-choice, and multiple-answer formats with original questions and official Microsoft Learn references.

items.prompt: An environment that uses private infrastructure together with public-cloud services is a {{blank}} cloud.
items.explanation: A hybrid cloud intentionally connects or coordinates private or on-premises resources with public-cloud services. Serverless and SaaS are consumption or service models, not the deployment model spanning both environments.

items.prompt: For each responsibility, decide whether it normally belongs to Microsoft when a customer uses Azure Software as a Service.

statements.text: Maintain the physical datacenter
Decide which employees can access the customer’s data
Classify the customer’s business records

items.explanation: Microsoft operates the cloud facilities and service platform. The customer still governs its identities, access, information classification, and appropriate use even when the complete application is provider managed.

items.prompt: Which TWO statements accurately describe a consumption-based cloud model?
answers: Monthly charges can change with usage
Resources can be released when they are no longer required
options: Monthly charges can change with usage
Resources can be released when they are no longer required
All capacity becomes a customer-owned capital asset
Every workload has the same fixed price regardless of consumption
items.explanation: Consumption pricing meters eligible resource use rather than requiring ownership of maximum capacity. This allows spending to vary and lets teams stop paying for released resources, subject to each service’s pricing terms.

items.prompt: A bank operates infrastructure dedicated to the bank in its own datacenter. Which deployment model does this describe?
items.explanation: A private cloud is dedicated to one organization and can be located in its own facilities. No public-cloud component is described, and PaaS identifies a service type rather than infrastructure ownership.

items.prompt: A company moves from buying servers to paying monthly for Azure usage. How is the cloud spending normally categorized?
options: Datacenter depreciation owned by Microsoft’s customer
items.explanation: Pay-as-you-go cloud service costs are operating expenses tied to consumption. Purchasing hardware creates a capital asset, which is the up-front model the company is replacing.

items.prompt: Increasing the CPU and memory of one virtual machine is {{blank}} scaling.
items.explanation: Vertical scaling changes the capacity of an existing instance. Horizontal scaling changes the number of instances, while geographic and serverless do not name this resize direction.

references.title: Describe the benefits of using cloud services

items.prompt: Evaluate each example of cloud predictability and governance.

statements.text: Performance test results can help forecast capacity needs
A cost estimate guarantees the exact future invoice
Central policy can reduce configuration inconsistency

items.explanation: Predictability uses evidence to improve forecasts but cannot guarantee an exact future bill because usage and prices can vary. Governance applies organizational standards across resources.

references.title: Describe the benefits of using cloud services

items.prompt: An application is deployed across fault-isolated locations so that it can recover from a location failure. Which benefit is the main goal?
items.explanation: Reliability is the ability to recover from failures and continue operating as designed. Financial treatment, authentication, and data labels do not describe the resilient architecture.

references.title: Describe the benefits of using cloud services

items.prompt: Administrators use a portal, command line, APIs, and templates to control the same cloud resources. Which benefit is illustrated?
items.explanation: Multiple consistent management interfaces make resources easier to provision, configure, and automate. They do not establish who owns hardware, whether infrastructure is dedicated, or where data must reside.

references.title: Describe the benefits of using cloud services

items.prompt: {{blank}} describes a complete subscription CRM application managed by its provider.
items.explanation: SaaS provides the finished application and manages its underlying stack. IaaS and PaaS give customers infrastructure or an application platform on which they operate more of the solution.

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about customer control in cloud service types.

statements.text: IaaS gives a customer control of the guest operating system
PaaS requires the customer to maintain the physical hosts
SaaS gives the customer administrator access to the provider’s application servers

items.explanation: Customers manage the guest OS in IaaS. Providers operate physical infrastructure in every cloud service type, and SaaS users configure the application without administering its underlying servers.

items.prompt: Which TWO scenarios are best matched to platform as a service (PaaS)?
answers: Deploy a web app while the provider patches the runtime
Use a managed database while controlling schemas and data
options: Deploy a web app while the provider patches the runtime
Use a managed database while controlling schemas and data
Install a custom kernel driver on a guest operating system
Subscribe to a finished payroll application
items.explanation: PaaS manages the runtime or database platform while customers focus on their applications and data. Guest OS customization points to IaaS, and a complete payroll product is SaaS.

items.prompt: A lift-and-shift migration places an unchanged legacy server on an Azure virtual machine. Which service type is used?
items.explanation: An Azure virtual machine exposes a customer-managed guest operating system, so it is IaaS. The application has not been redesigned for a managed platform or replaced by a provider application.

items.prompt: In which service type does the provider manage the application, runtime, operating system, and physical infrastructure?
items.explanation: SaaS places the complete application stack under provider operation, while customers manage their data, users, and configuration. IaaS and PaaS leave the customer responsible for the application.

items.prompt: The Azure hierarchy from broad to narrow is management group, subscription, resource group, and {{blank}}.
items.explanation: An Azure resource is the manageable item inside a resource group. Availability zones and region pairs are physical-resiliency concepts, while a tenant is an identity boundary rather than the next child in this hierarchy.

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about Azure regions and availability zones.

statements.text: An Azure region contains one or more datacenters
Every Azure region supports availability zones
An availability zone is a billing container above a subscription

items.explanation: Regions are geographic deployment areas containing datacenters. Zone availability varies by region and service, and a zone is physical infrastructure—not a subscription or billing hierarchy scope.

items.prompt: Several application resources are deployed in different Azure regions but share one lifecycle. Can they be placed in one resource group?
items.answer: Yes, a resource group can contain resources from different regions
options: Yes, a resource group can contain resources from different regions
No, a resource group is an availability zone
No, every resource group is limited to one datacenter
Yes, but only if every resource has the same type
items.explanation: A resource group is a logical management container and can contain different resource types and locations. Its own stored metadata location does not force all contained resources into that region.

items.prompt: Governance must apply to four subscriptions without assigning the same rule four times. Where should the subscriptions be organized?
items.explanation: A policy or access assignment at a management group can inherit to subscriptions beneath it. Resource groups cannot contain subscriptions, and availability sets or networks are workload constructs.

items.prompt: A managed service for publishing web apps and REST APIs is Azure App {{blank}}.
items.explanation: Azure App Service hosts web applications and APIs on a managed platform with deployment and scaling features. The other completions refer to network, observability, or storage capabilities.

references.title: Describe Azure compute and networking services

items.prompt: For each connectivity requirement, decide whether Azure ExpressRoute is the best match.

statements.text: Use a provider connection that avoids the public internet
Connect two Azure virtual networks with no circuit provider
Resolve a public domain name to an IP address

items.explanation: ExpressRoute supplies private connectivity from an on-premises network through a connectivity provider. Virtual network peering joins Azure networks, and Azure DNS performs name resolution.

references.title: Describe Azure compute and networking services

items.prompt: Which TWO compute options package application dependencies in containers rather than requiring a full guest operating system for every instance?
items.explanation: Container Instances and AKS both run container images, with AKS adding Kubernetes orchestration. Virtual Desktop supplies desktop sessions, and VPN Gateway connects networks instead of hosting application containers.

references.title: Describe Azure compute and networking services

items.prompt: Remote staff need personal and pooled Windows desktops delivered from Azure. Which service addresses the requirement?
items.explanation: Azure Virtual Desktop delivers cloud-hosted Windows desktops and applications to users. Functions runs event code, DNS resolves names, and Files supplies shared storage.

references.title: Describe Azure compute and networking services

items.prompt: A branch office needs an encrypted tunnel to an Azure virtual network over its existing internet connection. What should it use?
items.explanation: VPN Gateway supports site-to-site encrypted connectivity across the public internet. ExpressRoute uses a private provider circuit, peering connects Azure virtual networks, and Policy governs configuration.

references.title: Describe Azure compute and networking services

items.prompt: Azure {{blank}} provides a shared SMB file system that cloud and on-premises clients can mount.
items.explanation: Azure Files provides managed file shares accessible through protocols such as SMB. Queues exchange messages, Tables store key-value entities, and managed disks attach block storage to virtual machines.

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about Blob Storage access tiers.

statements.text: The hot tier is designed for frequently accessed data
The archive tier offers immediate low-latency reads
Changing tiers can affect storage and access charges

items.explanation: Hot storage favors frequent access, while archive data must be rehydrated before reading and can take hours. Tier selection trades storage price against access, transaction, and retrieval characteristics.

items.prompt: Which TWO redundancy options copy data to a secondary Azure region?
items.explanation: GRS and GZRS add asynchronous replication to a secondary region. LRS remains within one datacenter, while ZRS spreads synchronous copies across zones in one region.

items.prompt: An application needs a durable queue so components can exchange messages asynchronously. Which storage service should it use?
items.explanation: Queue Storage holds messages that decouple application components and can be processed asynchronously. Disks and Files store block or file data, while DNS performs name resolution.

items.prompt: Which redundancy choice writes synchronous copies across availability zones in the primary region and also replicates to a secondary region?
items.explanation: GZRS combines zonal protection in the primary region with geographic replication to a secondary region. ZRS lacks the secondary copy, GRS does not use primary-region zones, and premium is a performance tier.

items.prompt: {{blank}} uses signals such as user, device compliance, location, and risk to control sign-in.
items.explanation: Conditional Access evaluates identity and contextual signals to require controls such as MFA or block access. Domain Services supplies managed domain features, while storage and cost tools do not make sign-in decisions.

references.title: Describe Azure identity, access, and security

items.prompt: Which TWO capabilities can reduce reliance on reusable passwords?
items.explanation: MFA adds another proof to a password-based flow, and passwordless methods can replace the password with stronger factors. Tags and lifecycle policies manage resource metadata or data retention.

references.title: Describe Azure identity, access, and security

items.prompt: Users sign in once and then open several authorized applications without another prompt. Which capability is being used?
items.explanation: SSO lets an authenticated identity access multiple authorized applications without repeating the entire sign-in process. The alternatives concern storage, networking, and resource protection.

references.title: Describe Azure identity, access, and security

items.prompt: A legacy application needs LDAP, Kerberos, and managed domain join, but the team does not want to deploy domain controllers. Which service fits?
items.explanation: Microsoft Entra Domain Services provides managed domain capabilities compatible with many legacy workloads. RBAC authorizes Azure resource actions, while health and storage services solve unrelated problems.

references.title: Describe Azure identity, access, and security

items.prompt: Which security strategy uses controls at physical, identity, perimeter, network, compute, application, and data layers?
items.explanation: Defense in depth layers safeguards so one failed control does not expose the entire system. Pricing, scaling, and grouping do not describe a multilayer security model.

references.title: Describe Azure identity, access, and security

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about Azure cost planning and analysis.

statements.text: The Pricing calculator estimates a proposed deployment
Cost analysis can report actual recorded charges
A resource tag automatically discounts the tagged service

items.explanation: The calculator models expected prices before deployment, while Cost Management analyzes actual and forecast spend. Tags add allocation metadata but do not alter a service’s rate or usage.

items.prompt: Which TWO factors can change the price of an Azure solution even when its application code is unchanged?
answers: The Azure region selected for resources
The amount of outbound data transferred
options: The Azure region selected for resources
The amount of outbound data transferred
The display name of a resource group
The order in which tags are entered
items.explanation: Azure service rates can vary by region, and outbound transfer can be metered. Names and tag ordering are management metadata with no direct price effect.

items.prompt: A team wants an email when forecast monthly spending approaches a target. What should it configure?
items.answer: A Cost Management budget with alert conditions
options: A Cost Management budget with alert conditions
items.explanation: Budgets compare actual or forecast cost with defined thresholds and can trigger notifications. Locks, availability sets, and private endpoints do not monitor aggregate spending.

items.prompt: A stable eligible workload will run continuously for three years. Which option may lower its compute price in exchange for a commitment?
items.explanation: A reservation can provide discounted eligible usage for a committed term. Tags organize resources, endpoints provide connectivity, and locks protect changes without purchasing capacity.

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about resource locks.

statements.text: A delete lock can prevent an authorized user from deleting a resource
A read-only lock allows ordinary update operations
A lock grants a user permission to view a resource

items.explanation: Locks restrict control-plane changes even when RBAC would otherwise allow them. Read-only locks block updates, and locks never grant identity permissions; access must come from authorization such as RBAC.

references.title: Describe Azure governance and compliance tools

items.prompt: Which TWO capabilities are associated with Microsoft Purview?
answers: Classify sensitive information across a data estate
options: Classify sensitive information across a data estate
items.explanation: Purview supports data governance through discovery, cataloging, classification, and lineage. Compute scaling and network tunneling are performed by other Azure services.

references.title: Describe Azure governance and compliance tools

items.prompt: An audit needs a list of resources that violate an assigned secure-transfer rule. Which capability supplies the result?
items.explanation: Azure Policy evaluates resources against assigned definitions and reports compliant and noncompliant results. DNS, reservations, and peering do not assess resource configuration rules.

references.title: Describe Azure governance and compliance tools

items.prompt: What is the key distinction between Azure Policy and Azure RBAC?
items.answer: Policy evaluates resource state; RBAC authorizes user and service actions
options: Policy evaluates resource state; RBAC authorizes user and service actions
Policy manages DNS; RBAC selects storage tiers
Policy calculates bills; RBAC creates budgets
They provide identical controls under different names
items.explanation: Policy governs whether configurations meet rules, while RBAC governs which management operations an identity may perform at a scope. They complement rather than replace each other.

references.title: Describe Azure governance and compliance tools

items.prompt: A Linux administrator wants cross-platform commands such as az group create. Which tool should be installed?
items.explanation: Azure CLI uses az commands and works across common operating systems. Azure PowerShell uses cmdlets, while Advisor and Policy are cloud services rather than local command syntaxes.

references.title: Describe tools for managing and deploying Azure resources

items.prompt: An administrator prefers object-based pipelines and commands such as New-AzResourceGroup. Which tool matches that preference?
items.explanation: Azure PowerShell supplies Az-module cmdlets and PowerShell objects for managing Azure. AzCopy transfers storage data, while DNS and Purview are services managed by such tools.

references.title: Describe tools for managing and deploying Azure resources

items.prompt: A Bicep deployment runs again with no desired-state changes. What result should a declarative deployment aim for?
items.answer: Keep resources in the declared state without duplicating them
options: Keep resources in the declared state without duplicating them
Create a second copy of every resource
Move all resources to a different tenant
items.explanation: Declarative infrastructure describes the intended end state, allowing repeated deployment to converge on that state. An unchanged definition should not imply duplicate infrastructure or unrelated destructive moves.

references.title: Describe tools for managing and deploying Azure resources

items.prompt: What file format is used by an Azure Resource Manager template?
items.explanation: ARM templates are declarative JSON documents describing Azure resources and dependencies. Bicep offers another authoring syntax but compiles to the ARM JSON representation.

references.title: Describe tools for managing and deploying Azure resources

items.prompt: An administrator wants a graphical browser interface for creating and managing Azure resources. Which tool is the direct fit?
items.explanation: The Azure portal is the browser-based graphical management interface. AzCopy and Data Box move data, while Log Analytics is used to query monitoring logs.

references.title: Describe tools for managing and deploying Azure resources

items.prompt: Which Azure Monitor data type stores time-stamped numeric measurements such as CPU percentage?
items.explanation: Metrics are lightweight numerical time-series values suitable for charts and threshold alerts. Locks, tags, and management groups are resource-management constructs, not telemetry records.

items.prompt: A team needs to query collected logs by using Kusto Query Language. Which Azure Monitor experience should it use?
items.explanation: Log Analytics provides KQL queries over Azure Monitor Logs workspaces. Advisor recommends improvements, the calculator estimates prices, and DNS resolves names.

items.prompt: Which service reports planned maintenance and health advisories relevant to a company’s Azure subscriptions?
items.explanation: Service Health supplies personalized notices for subscribed services and regions, including maintenance and advisories. The public status page is broad and not tailored to the customer’s resources.

items.prompt: A team receives an Azure Advisor recommendation. What should it assume?
items.answer: The recommendation should be reviewed and is not automatically implemented in every case
options: The recommendation should be reviewed and is not automatically implemented in every case
Advisor has already changed every affected resource
The recommendation is an active platform outage
Advisor has granted the team new RBAC permissions
items.explanation: Advisor analyzes configuration and proposes optimizations for review. It does not universally make the change, declare incidents, or alter identity permissions merely by issuing a recommendation.
